minSerializerVersionNote: ENGINE FLOOR (base >= 3.0.0): Truvio.Commerce.Serializer >= 0.8.1-beta — the first engine retargeted to DW 10.28.1-PreRelease (Swift 2.4 roll-forward). Carries everything the 0.8.0-beta floor guaranteed (render-time page/grid-row/paragraph permission blocks + deferred permission re-apply + user-group cache refresh). Proven on DW 10.28.1-PreRelease; stable re-prove due when DW 10.28 lands stable on NuGet.
description: Machine-readable guarantees the base layer makes to every addition (v3.0 Distribution, base 3.0.0 FRAMEWORK-ONLY). The Swift 2.4 base split moved ALL content (areas 3 + 27, both mode trees) and UrlPath to the surface-swift layer; the base now ships only the framework SQL sets + this contract + SQL-predicate config. Additions (feature/sample-data/surface/theme layers) bind ONLY to this contract - never to each other. Content-scoped contract bits (content areas, langPrefix, navDepth, page anchors, title rules, per-environment Area exclusions, protected Swift item types) moved to layers/surface-swift/surface.contract-notes.json. The gate reads this file for the base-contract collision check.

idRules.nvarcharPkNote: When an addition writes rows into a base-owned table whose PK is nvarchar (most Ecom tables - PriceId, PaymentId, ProductId, etc.), it MUST namespace the PK with a PACK-<NAME>- prefix (uppercase layer name). Keeps additions disjoint from the base and from each other.
idRules.intIdentityNote: When an addition writes rows keyed by an int-identity PK, it MUST reserve an id at or above the 100000 floor. Applies equally to item-instance ids (ItemType_<systemName> PK rows).
idRules.baseCatalogNote: The base ships ZERO catalog (scaffolding-only, swift/2.3.2): EcomGroups, EcomProducts, EcomGroupProductRelation, EcomVariantGroups, EcomVariantsOptions, EcomVariantOptionsProductRelation, EcomDiscount, EcomDiscountTranslation, EcomPrices carry no base rows. The collision surface for these tables is addition-vs-sample-data-vs-addition, arbitrated statically by the gate.
idRules.reservedFixtureNote: The sample-data layer's demo catalog (merge/_sql/catalog.sql) reserves these key prefixes. Additions must not use them.
idRules.noPerItemTypeNumericTableNote: The contract is prefix-based, not per-itemType-numeric. There is deliberately NO per-itemType numeric range table - do not invent one.

guaranteedRows.permissionGroupsNote: DW 10.26.9 has no AccessUserGroup table - groups ARE AccessUser rows of type 2.

referenceCategory.note: Hidden template category (CategoryType 2) required by DemoVerifier Check 2. Seeded pre-host-start (DW caches at startup).

contractPrice.note: Ships in the sample-data layer's catalog.sql, next to the product it scopes to; present only when an edition activates sampleData: true.

anchors.note: Content anchors (areas 3/27, langPrefix /swift-2) moved to surface-swift (base 3.0.0 ships zero content areas).

repositories.name: Products
repositories.note: The base guarantees the Products repository/index/query/facets are provisioned by the gate. 'ProductsFrontend' is dead/removed - never provisioned by the clean-room.

replaceFiltered.note: FILTER-01: only the three permission groups are base-owned on AccessUser; user rows (1328/1326) are seeded, not serialized into the base.

baseOwnedTables.note: Additions touching a base-owned table follow idRules (prefix / floor) and must pass the static intersection checks below. Base ships zero catalog (see idRules.baseCatalog) and zero content areas (base split 3.0.0). UrlPath is surface-swift-owned (its single row is a friendly-URL redirect bound to area 3 + a Swift page id - no route targets exist in a framework-only base).

collisionChecks.rule: Intersect an addition's _sql/<Table>/*.yml filenames (= row keys; composite keys join with $$) with the base's filenames for the same table. Non-empty = FAIL.
WR-10: the same table+filename shipped by >1 active addition = FAIL. A single layer shipping the same key in its own replace+merge is NOT a collision.

07-01: an addition's fragment-shipped _content relative paths must be disjoint from base _content paths (union of both mode trees); cross-layer parity; shipped-files-only (referenced base anchors excluded); declared-footprint parity (every Content entry matches a declared fragmentContent {areaId, path}).

WR-04: (itemType, fields.Id) pairs land as PK rows in shared ItemType_<systemName> tables. The same pair by >1 owner = FAIL. Reusing an id NUMBER across DIFFERENT itemType tables is fine (PK surface is per-table).

registrationAsymmetry.rule: Every csLedger entry with registration 'addin-attribute-config-row' MUST have >=1 matching configRows entry.
registrationAsymmetry.note: Cross-field rule enforced at activation (not JSON-Schema-expressible); locked by tests.

movedToSurfaceSwift.note: Moved by the base split (Swift 2.4 roll-forward): perEnvironmentAreaExclusions, protectedItemTypes, content anchors (areas/langPrefix), navDepth obligation, title-integrity rules. See layers/surface-swift/surface.contract-notes.json.
